prompt: In diraclang, print 'Hello, World!'.
completion: <dirac>  <output>Hello, World!</output> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, if num is greater than 10, print 'Value is large'; otherwise, print 'Value is small'.
completion: <dirac>  <defvar name="num" value="5" />  <test-if test="$num > 10">    <output>Value is large</output>  </test-if>  <test-if test="$num <= 10">    <output>Value is small</output>  </test-if> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, loop from 0 to 2 and print each value.
completion: <dirac>  <defvar name="i" value="0" />  <loop count="3">    <output><variable name="i" /></output>    <assign name="i"><eval><variable name="i" /> + 1</eval></assign>  </loop> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, define a variable myvar with value 'test123' and print it.
completion: <dirac>  <defvar name="myvar" value="test123" />  <output>Value is: <variable name="myvar" /> </output> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, define variables a=10, b=5, and print their sum as 'Result: 15'.
completion: <dirac>  <defvar name="a" value="10" />  <defvar name="b" value="5" />  <output>Result: </output>  <expr eval="plus">    <arg><variable name="a" /></arg>    <arg><variable name="b" /></arg>  </expr> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, define a variable x=5, use an if conditional to check if x equals 5, print 'Match!' if true, 'No match!' if false.
completion: <dirac>  <defvar name="x" value="5" />  <if>    <cond eval="eq">      <arg><variable name="x" /></arg>      <arg>5</arg>    </cond>    <then>      <output>Match!</output>    </then>    <else>      <output>No match!</output>    </else>  </if> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, throw an error and catch it, then print 'Caught error'.
completion: <dirac>  <try>    <eval>throw new Error("Test error");</eval>  </try>  <catch>    <output>Caught error</output>  </catch> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, write two lines to a file, output to stdout, verify file and clean up.
completion: <dirac>  <output file="output-test-temp.txt">File line 1</output>  <output file="output-test-temp.txt">File line 2</output>  <output>stdout output</output>  <system shell="bash">    if [ -f output-test-temp.txt ]; then      echo "File written"      rm output-test-temp.txt    fi  </system> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, import a math library and call SQUARE with x=5.
completion: <dirac>  <import src="../examples/lib/math.di" />  <call name="SQUARE" x="5" /> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, define a subroutine fullname with first and last name, and print 'John Doe'.
completion: <dirac>  <subroutine name="fullname" param-first="string" param-last="string">    <eval name="result">return first + ' ' + last;</eval>    <output><variable name="result" /></output>  </subroutine>    <call name="fullname" first="John" last="Doe" /> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, define a parent subroutine without visible, call nested, and print cleanup message.
completion: <subroutine name="PARENT_WITHOUT_VISIBLE">  <output>Parent without visible called</output>    <subroutine name="SHOULD_BE_CLEANED">    <output>This should not be called</output>  </subroutine> </subroutine>  <PARENT_WITHOUT_VISIBLE />  <output>After call: nested was cleaned up</output>

prompt: In diraclang, print 'Hello World' without a root element.
completion: <output>Hello</output> <output> </output> <output>World</output>

prompt: In diraclang, generate a script, write to file, execute, and print result.
completion:
<dirac>  <defvar name="generated_script" literal="true" trim="false">
<dirac>  <defvar name="value">42</defvar>  <output>Result: <variable name="value" /></output>
</dirac>  </defvar>  <output file="temp-generated.di"><variable name="generated_script" /></output>  <output>Generated file executed</output>  <system shell="bash">    node dist/cli.js temp-generated.di    rm temp-generated.di  </system> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, echo hello using system command.
completion: <dirac>  <system>echo hello</system> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, import a library with variable substitution and double 10.
completion: <dirac>  <subroutine name="TestSuite">    <defvar name="libPath">./fixtures/test-lib.di</defvar>    <import src="${libPath}" />    <defvar name="result" trim="true"><DOUBLE x="10" /></defvar>    <output><variable name="result" /></output>  </subroutine>  <TestSuite /> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, set background color to blue using llm and validate.
completion: <dirac>  <subroutine name="set-color" description="Set a background color" param-color="string:required:Color name">    <parameters select="@color"/>    <output>Background color is: <variable name="color" /></output>  </subroutine>  <llm execute="true" validate="true" maxTokens="100">    Set the background color to blue  </llm> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, break a loop at 3 and print after loop.
completion: <loop count="10">  <test-if test="$i" ge="3">    <output>Breaking at <variable name="i" /></output>    <break />  </test-if>  <output>Loop: <variable name="i" /></output> </loop> <output>After loop</output>

prompt: In diraclang, check if PATH environment variable exists and print result.
completion: <dirac>  <defvar name="mypath">    <environment name="PATH" />  </defvar>  <output>Path exists: </output>  <test-if test="$mypath != ''">    <output>true</output>  </test-if>  <test-if test="$mypath == ''">    <output>false</output>  </test-if> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, read all stdin and print it.
completion: <dirac>  <defvar name="data">    <input source="stdin" mode="all"/>  </defvar>  <output><variable name="data"/></output> </dirac>

prompt: In diraclang, test if x is greater than 5 and print 'Greater!'.
completion: <dirac>  <defvar name="x" value="10" />  <test-if test="$x" gt="5">    <output>Greater!</output>  </test-if> </dirac>
